title: Corollary 'exact-slice': vanishing over PROPER H uses wrong threshold (r_H vs r_G)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap (specific to the current revised solution)

Corollary `cor:exact-slice` claims $\Sigma^k HM$ is **exactly** an $\mathcal O$-$(kr_G)$-slice, where $r_G=[G:\chi^{\mathcal O}(G)]$. The upper-truncation half requires that ALL generators of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge kr_G+1}$ map trivially into $\Sigma^k HM$, i.e. for every $H\subseteq G$ and every admissible $T\in\mathcal O(H)$ with $|T|>kr_G$,
$$[G_+\otimes_H N^TS^1,\ \Sigma^kHM]^G \cong [N^TS^1,\ i_H^*\Sigma^kHM]^H = 0.$$

The proof dispatches this in one sentence: "By Lemma `lem:restr`(1)-(2) and Lemma `lem:geom`(3) applied over each $H$ (with $i_H^*\Sigma^k HM$, which is again of the form $\Sigma^k H(\text{geometric})$ over $H$), all maps from these generators into $\Sigma^k HM$ vanish." This is **not justified** and is likely false as stated:

1. **Geometricity does not restrict.** Lemma `lem:geom`(3) requires the target to be $\Sigma^k H M'$ with $M'$ a *geometric* Mackey functor for $(H,\mathcal O|_H)$, meaning (G1) $\widetilde{E\mathcal P_H}$-local and (G2) $\Phi^H(\,\cdot\,)$ an EM spectrum in degree 0. The hypothesis on $M$ is at the $G$-level: $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$-local and $\Phi^G HM$ EM in degree 0. There is no argument that $i_H^*HM$ is $\widetilde{E\mathcal P_H}$-local (the family of proper subgroups changes when passing to $H$) nor that $\Phi^H i_H^*HM$ is concentrated in degree 0. In fact $\Phi^H i_H^* HM$ can be a nonzero, non-EM spectrum.

2. **Threshold mismatch $r_H$ vs $r_G$.** Even granting geometricity over $H$, Lemma `lem:geom`(3) at $H$ gives vanishing only for $|T|>k\,r_H$ with $r_H=[H:\chi^{\mathcal O|_H}(H)]=[H:\chi^{\mathcal O}(H)]$ (Lemma `lem:restr`(1)). But the truncation $P^{kr_G}_{\mathcal O}$ provides cells only with $|T|>k r_G$. When $H$ is a proper subgroup with $r_H<r_G$ (e.g. any proper $H$ with $\chi^{\mathcal O}(H)=H$, so $r_H=1$), the threshold $kr_G+1$ is FAR above $kr_H$, so the available constraint $|T|>kr_G$ does not even reach the regime $kr_H<|T|\le kr_G$ where $i_H^*\Sigma^kHM$ could have nonzero slices. The cited lemma simply does not apply at the needed degree.

## Why this matters
Corollary `cor:exact-slice` is invoked implicitly to justify that the geometric Postnikov layers $\Sigma^j HM_j$ are honest slices and that the filtration in the converse is the slice filtration. If the exactness fails at proper $H$, the identification of $\Sigma^kHM$ as a $(kr_G)$-slice is unproven, undermining the claim that the constructed tower is the $\mathcal O$-slice tower (as opposed to merely a tower with the right connectivity).

## Difficulty
This is the hardest genuine gap. Closing it requires either (i) proving that for proper $H$, $i_H^*\Sigma^kHM$ is $\widetilde{E\mathcal P_H}$-locally trivial or $\Phi^H$-trivial (so that no induced generator at proper $H$ maps in nontrivially), or (ii) reorganizing so that exactness of slices is never needed at proper $H$. A concrete acid test: verify the corollary directly for $G=C_{p^2}$, $\mathcal O=\{C_p\to C_{p^2}\}$ (so $\chi(G)=C_p$, $r_G=p$, but $r_{C_p}=1$), where the mismatch is maximal.
